county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1883, organized 1908. SW-central ND. Named for John Dunn, early settler. Manning is county seat. Oil production (Bakken formation). Fort Berthold Reservation partly in Dunn County.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Dunn County Recorder (Manning). Fort Berthold Indian Reservation (MHA Nation — Mandan, Hidatsa, Arikara/Three Affiliated Tribes) covers significant portion. Tribal enrollment records at Fort Berthold Agency and NARA Kansas City. Garrison Dam (1953) displaced tribal communities — relocation records at NARA. Oil boom county (Bakken formation since 2008). Pre-1908 records under Stark County. SHSND microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Germans from Russia and diverse settlers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Foster 1885. Central ND. Named for Ezra Eddy, territorial legislator. New Rockford is county seat. Norwegian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Eddy County Recorder (New Rockford). Small rural county organized 1885. Mixed Scandinavian and American-born settlement. New Rockford was a railroad town on the Northern Pacific. Eddy County Historical Society. Limited specialized resources; SHSND in Bismarck is primary archive. Check Lutheran church records in New Rockford and Sheyenne. James River Valley — overlaps with neighboring Foster and Wells counties for family research.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Norwegian immigrants.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1879, organized 1883. South-central ND — SD border. Named for James Emmons, territorial legislator. Linton is county seat. HUGE Germans from Russia settlement — one of most concentrated in ND. Streeter, Hazelton, Strasburg (Lawrence Welk's birthplace).
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Emmons County Recorder (Linton). Germans from Russia heartland — one of the most concentrated GR settlement areas in North America. GRHS in Bismarck has extensive Emmons County family files. Lawrence Welk birthplace in Strasburg. German-Russian Catholic and Evangelical church records are CRITICAL genealogical sources — parishes in Linton, Strasburg, Hague, Braddock, and Hazelton. Standing Rock Reservation borders southern edge. SHSND microfilm. Emmons County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Germans from Russia dominant. One of most concentrated GR communities.

source_profiles.source_name: Germans from Russia Heritage Society Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Germans from Russia Heritage Society in Bismarck. Church records, village histories, immigration records essential for Emmons County families. Many families from Black Sea and Volga German colonies.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873, organized 1883. Central ND. Named for James Foster, railroad superintendent. Carrington is county seat. Norwegian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Foster County Recorder (Carrington). Organized 1883. Carrington Research Extension Center (NDSU agriculture) — employment records may be relevant. Mixed Scandinavian and American-born settlement. Foster County Historical Society. Small county; SHSND is primary archive. Check Lutheran church records in Carrington, Grace City, and McHenry (town, not county). James River Valley families may also appear in Stutsman or Wells counties.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Norwegian immigrants.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. Young county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Billings 1912. SW ND — MT border. Named for the valley. Beach is county seat. Ranching and oil. Small population.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Golden Valley County Recorder (Beach). Formed 1912 from Billings. Western border county adjacent to Montana — some families filed records in both states. Badlands edge ranching territory. Sparse population with limited church and institutional records. Pre-1912 records under Billings County. SHSND is primary archive. Check Sentinel Butte and Beach community records. Golden Valley County Historical Society.

source_profiles.source_name: Golden Valley County Recorder — Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online land records via NDRIN or county portal.
Probate records.
Diverse ranching settlers.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known record losses. 1997 Red River flood damaged some modern records but historical records preserved.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created and organized 1873. Grand Forks is county seat — 3rd largest ND city. University of North Dakota. Named for the forks of the Red River. Red River Valley. Norwegian settlement dominant. 1997 flood devastated the city but courthouse records survived.
county_metadata.research_tips: FamilySearch and Grand Forks County Recorder (Grand Forks). Excellent online records portal. UND Chester Fritz Library — Elwyn B. Robinson Department of Special Collections holds regional manuscripts, Norwegian-American collections, and Icelandic materials. Grand Forks County Historical Society. Red River Valley — strong Norwegian heritage. City directories for Grand Forks from 1880s. 1997 flood damaged some county records — check SHSND for microfilm backups of pre-flood records. Grand Forks Public Library genealogy collection.

source_profiles.source_name: Grand Forks County Recorder — Land Records
